lines.english: I am meek; I have the support of the Lord, Har, Har. ||4||
Some say that they have the arms of their many brothers to protect them.
Some say that they have great expanses of wealth.
Some fast and take vows, and wear malas.
Some dance, wearing ankle bells.
Some apply ceremonial tilak marks to their foreheads.
Some work spells using the miraculous spiritual powers of the Siddhas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਹਿ ਦੀਨ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਧਿਆਇਆ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: mohi deen har har har dhiaaeaa |5|
lines.english: I am meek; I meditate on the Lord, Har, Har, Har. ||5||
Some perform Tantric spells, and chant various mantras.
Some wear various religious robes and establish their authority.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਹਿ ਦੀਨ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਸੇਵਾ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: mohi deen har har har sevaa |6|
lines.english: I am meek; I serve the Lord, Har, Har, Har. ||6||
One calls himself a wise Pandit, a religious scholar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਹਿ ਦੀਨ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਸਰਣੀ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: mohi deen har har har saranee |7|
lines.english: I am meek; I seek the Sanctuary of the Lord, Har, Har, Har. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋ ਖਟੁ ਕਰਮ ਸਹਿਤ ਸਿਉ ਮੰਡਿਤ ॥
lines.translit: ko khatt karam sahit siau manddit |
lines.english: One performs the six rituals to appease Shiva.
One maintains the rituals of pure lifestyle, and does good deeds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਨ ਪ੍ਰਬੋਧੇ ॥
lines.translit: bin naavai ihu man na prabodhe |
lines.english: Without the Name, this mind is not awakened.
I have studied the religions and rituals of all the ages.
Says Nanak, when I found the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy,

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸੁ ਪਾਨੀ ਤੇ ਜਿਨਿ ਤੂ ਘਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: eis paanee te jin too ghariaa |
lines.english: He created you out of this water.
Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:
my thirsty desires were satisfied, and I was totally cooled and soothed. ||8||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਤ ਗਰਭ ਮਹਿ ਜਿਨਿ ਤੂ ਰਾਖਿਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: maat garabh meh jin too raakhiaa |1|
lines.english: In your mother's womb, He preserved you. ||1||
From clay, He fashioned your body.
He blessed you with the light of reason and clear consciousness.
He gave you your mother and father;
Give up all others thoughts, O mind. ||1||Pause||
Contemplate your Savior Lord.
he gave you your charming children and siblings;

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਕਉ ਰਖਿ ਲੇਹੁ ਚੀਤਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: tis tthaakur kau rakh lehu cheetaa |2|
lines.english: enshrine that Lord and Master in your consciousness. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਦੀਏ ਤੁਧੁ ਬਨਿਤਾ ਅਰੁ ਮੀਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: jin dee tudh banitaa ar meetaa |
lines.english: he gave you your spouse and friends;
He gave you the priceless water;
He gave you the invaluable air;
He gave you burning fire;

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਕੀ ਰਹੁ ਮਨ ਸਰਨਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: tis tthaakur kee rahu man saranaa |3|
lines.english: let your mind remain in the Sanctuary of that Lord and Master. ||3||
He gave you the thirty-six varieties of tasty foods;

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਕੇ ਚਿਤਿ ਰਖੁ ਚਰਨਾ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: tis tthaakur ke chit rakh charanaa |4|
lines.english: enshrine in your consciousness the feet of that Lord and Master. ||4||
He gave you a place within to hold them;
He gave you the earth, and things to use;

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਰਨ ਚਲਨ ਕਉ ਸਿਰੁ ਕੀਨੋ ਮੇਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: charan chalan kau sir keeno meraa |
lines.english: He gave you feet to walk upon, and the crowning glory of your head;
He gave you hands to work with, and a nose and a tongue;

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੇਖਨ ਕਉ ਨੇਤ੍ਰ ਸੁਨਨ ਕਉ ਕਰਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: pekhan kau netr sunan kau karanaa |
lines.english: He gave you eyes to see, and ears to hear;
He transformed you from impure to pure;

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲ ਜੋਨਿ ਮਹਿ ਤੂ ਸਿਰਿ ਧਰਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: sagal jon meh too sir dhariaa |
lines.english: He installed you above the heads of all creatures;

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਤਿਸੁ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਕੇ ਪੂਜਹੁ ਪੈਰਾ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: man tis tthaakur ke poojahu pairaa |5|
lines.english: O mind, worship the Feet of that Lord and Master. ||5||
Your affairs shall be resolved, O mind, meditating on God. ||6||
Here and there, only the One God exists.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਬ ਤੂ ਸੀਝੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਨਹੀ ਸੀਝੈ ॥
lines.translit: ab too seejh bhaavai nahee seejhai |
lines.english: now, you may fulfill your destiny or not;

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਤ ਕਤ ਦੇਖੀਐ ਤਤ ਤਤ ਤੋਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: jat kat dekheeai tat tat tohee |
lines.english: Wherever I look, there You are.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸੁ ਵਿਸਰਿਐ ਇਕ ਨਿਮਖ ਨ ਸਰੈ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: jis visariai ik nimakh na sarai |7|
lines.english: forgetting Him, I cannot survive, even for an instant. ||7||
My mind is reluctant to serve Him;
I am a sinner, without any virtue at all.
I do not serve You, or do any good deeds.
By great good fortune, I have found the boat - the Guru.
Some pass their lives enjoying pleasures and beauty.
Slave Nanak has crossed over, with Him. ||8||2||
Raamkalee, Fifth Mehl:
